attempts.test_code:
import pytest
from source import find_in_sorted

def test_value_greater_than_all_elements_no_infinite_recursion():
    # Bug: binsearch(mid, end) instead of binsearch(mid + 1, end).
    # When end - start == 1, mid == start, so binsearch(mid, end) == binsearch(start, end)
    # — the same call forever.  Triggered here because 8 > arr[4]=7 at binsearch(4, 5),
    # so the buggy code never advances and hits RecursionError.
    # Fixed code calls binsearch(5, 5) → start == end → returns -1.
    try:
        result = find_in_sorted([3, 4, 5, 6, 7], 8)
    except RecursionError:
        pytest.fail(
            "Infinite recursion: when x > arr[mid] and end - start == 1, "
            "binsearch(mid, end) repeats the same call instead of advancing to binsearch(mid + 1, end)"
        )
    assert result == -1, (
        f"find_in_sorted([3, 4, 5, 6, 7], 8) should return -1 (8 not in array), got {result}"
    )
